triumvirate_check: S65 P3 (CI flake-harden, Micro) verified. Quarantined the 4-peer real-WebRTC nplayer test to a non-gating CI lane so gating-CI-red == real regression. A.0: playwright.config retries=2-in-CI already exists (insufficient); deterministic transport REJECTED by prior Council (config C9); e2e.yml already deploy-independent. FIX: e2e.yml Run-E2E split into (1) GATING playwright test --grep-invert @quarantine-flaky (24 tests; reds the job on a real regression) + (2) NON-GATING continue-on-error+if:always --grep @quarantine-flaky --output=test-results-quarantine --reporter=list (the 1 tagged test; runs+visible but cannot red the gate; separate output dir + list reporter so it never overwrites the gating report/traces). priorities.label: S65 P1 (Micro) Lobby status-color landmine fixes. lobbyStateMachine.ts: JOIN_ATTEMPT-invalid -> statusColor=ERROR (fixes #1 grey-invalid); HOST_START + JOIN_ATTEMPT-valid -> statusColor=NORMAL + errorLatched=false (fixes #2 stale-red bleed + coupled Begin-block; color-only rejected as incoherent grey-but-latched). NOT touched: PEER_STATUS hot-path/churn-guard, ERROR, RESET, lobbyView, status TEXT literals, lobbyScreen.ts (applies v.statusColor unconditionally), e2e (asserts text not fill). Tests: rewrite invalid-no-recolour -> invalid-from-fresh-grey=red (#1 witness) + keep invalid-after-error; flip 2 keeps-red tests -> NORMAL + errorLatched=false; add HOST_START-after-ERROR-clears-latch-Begin-shows witness; keep churn/PEER/RESET/canary. Gate: tsc + full unit 985+ + e2e literal-sweep + full Playwright 24/1 + build<550. CHECK RALPH+GROK-ANALYST. Sanctioned: S64 logged these as fix-when-wanted.
priorities.check_method: S65 P1 (lobby status-colour landmine fixes, Micro) verified. Fixed both S64-logged landmines in the pure LobbyStateMachine reducer: (#1) invalid JOIN_ATTEMPT -> statusColor=ERROR (an invalid room code renders error-red not neutral grey); (#2) HOST_START + valid JOIN_ATTEMPT reset ALL prior session state (statusColor=NORMAL, errorLatched=false, beginVisible=false, hostConnected=false) so a stale error-red cannot bleed onto the next neutral status, a stale errorLatched cannot block Begin, and a stale Begin button cannot show on a freshly-hosted room. No-op in the normal select->host/join flow; only corrects the error-retry path. lobbyScreen.ts UNCHANGED (applies v.statusColor on every transition via applyView; RALPH verified the wiring on all 5 dispatch sites). e2e UNCHANGED (literal-sweep: lobby status COLOUR is asserted by no Playwright test - the 0xff3b6b hits are the in-game player palette). EMPIRICAL: tsc -b clean; full unit 985->988 (+3 witnesses); build 509.31KB (<550); full Playwright 24 pass/1 skip (incl protocol-mismatch S55 latch consumers + lobby Begin-gating + 4-peer nplayer passed @36s, no flake). CHECK = RALPH:PATROL + GROK-ANALYST (Micro tier). RALPH PASS (scope=2 src files, shell wiring verified, all invariants). GROK-ANALYST round-1 FAIL->FIXED: caught HOST_START/valid-JOIN spreading stale beginVisible/hostConnected=true after a host+peer+ERROR retry (my errorLatched-clear UNMASKED the pre-existing latent state) -> fixed by resetting all session-scoped fields + a witness test encoding the exact counterexample; round-2 verified the fix complete + no new regression. RESIDUAL (logged carry-forward, NOT dropped): invalid JOIN_ATTEMPT is not mode-guarded - reachable only via the dimmed join button while hosting (HTML input is display:none outside select, A.0-probed), pre-existing + low-severity; proper fix = shell/reducer mode-guard, own PDR. VERDICT SHIP. checkpoint d6b00ae.

verification.needle: fresh attempt RESETS all session state
an invalid code recolours the status to error-red

priorities.label: S65 P2 (Micro) Mode-guard invalid JOIN_ATTEMPT - close the P1 CHECK residual. Invalid JOIN_ATTEMPT dispatched while mode!=select (reachable via the dimmed joinButton; the HTML input is display:none outside select) paints a red JOIN_INVALID status in the wrong mode, and a later PEER_STATUS leaves the stale red. FIX: reducer JOIN_ATTEMPT guards mode!==select -> return state (no-op; behaviour-exact for all reachable flows since the UI only dispatches JOIN from select) + shell attemptJoin early-returns when mode!=select (no wasted dispatch). Tests: JOIN_ATTEMPT(valid+invalid) while hosting = same-ref no-op (no colour/mode change). Gate: tsc + full unit 988+ + full Playwright 24/1 + build<550. CHECK RALPH+GROK-ANALYST.
priorities.check_method: S65 P2 (mode-guard JOIN_ATTEMPT, Micro) verified. Closed the P1 CHECK residual: invalid JOIN_ATTEMPT dispatched while mode!=select (reachable via the dimmed joinButton; HTML input display:none outside select, Enter select-gated) painted a wrong-mode red JOIN_INVALID status, and a stale valid code could abandon a host session. FIX (defense-in-depth): reducer JOIN_ATTEMPT no-ops same-ref unless mode===select; shell attemptJoin early-returns unless mode===select. Behaviour-exact for all reachable flows (UI only dispatches JOIN from select). EMPIRICAL: tsc clean; full unit 988->992 (+4 witnesses: invalid/valid-while-hosting same-ref no-op, while-joining no-op, from-select still acts); build 509.37KB (<550); full Playwright 24 pass/1 skip (join-from-select unaffected; protocol-mismatch + lobby specs green). CHECK = RALPH:PATROL PASS (scope=3 files, 47 ins/0 del pure additions) + GROK-ANALYST PASS (3 attacks - stale-input button-spam during host transition, RESET->select race, direct reducer call from a future network/retry path - all held; no legitimate non-select JOIN flow; same-ref correct; guards do not conflict; dual-guard redundant-but-harmless defense-in-depth). VERDICT SHIP. checkpoint 3f5c33b.

verification.needle: JOIN_ATTEMPT is mode-guarded (acts ONLY from select)

priorities.label: S65 P3 (Micro) CI flake-harden - quarantine the 4-peer real-WebRTC nplayer test to a non-gating lane so gating-CI-red == real regression. A.0: playwright.config retries=2-in-CI already exists (insufficient: 90s timeout x3 still reds); deterministic-transport REJECTED by prior Council (config C9: real Trystero/Nostr IS the tested surface); e2e.yml already deploy-independent. FIX (e2e.yml only, minimal blast radius): split Run-E2E into (1) gating: playwright test --grep-invert "4-player FFA" (a real regression reds the job) + (2) non-gating continue-on-error:true: --grep "4-player FFA" (the flaky 4-peer runs + is visible but does NOT gate). N-player correctness stays gating via nplayerSeating units + the stable fast 6-player-render e2e. Gate: YAML parse + LOCAL grep-partition proof + full e2e. CHECK RALPH+GROK-ANALYST + Rule22 runtime audit.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #lobby-colour-landmine-fix #fresh-attempt-resets-all-session-state #grok-check-caught-incomplete-fix: fixed the 2 S64-logged lobby status-colour landmines in the pure reducer - (#1) invalid room code now renders error-red not neutral grey (JOIN_ATTEMPT invalid -> statusColor=ERROR); (#2) HOST_START + valid JOIN_ATTEMPT now reset ALL session state (grey + unlatch + beginVisible/hostConnected false) so a stale error-red cannot bleed onto a neutral status and a stale errorLatched cannot block Begin. KEY (Grok earned its keep AGAIN, cf S64 colour divergence): my first fix reset only colour+latch; the GROK-ANALYST break-it lens found HOST_START still spread stale beginVisible/hostConnected=true after a host+peer+ERROR retry -> a Begin button on a fresh 0-peer room; my own errorLatched-clear UNMASKED this pre-existing latent state (previously frozen behind the latch). FIXED by resetting all session-scoped fields on a fresh attempt (incoherent tuple unrepresentable); Grok round-2 verified clean. LESSON: a PARTIAL fresh-start reset is itself a divergence - when you clear SOME prior-state fields, clear ALL of them, or the unmasked remainder is worse than the original landmine. A.0 reachability probe (input display:none outside select) proved the residual cross-pane invalid-JOIN glitch degenerate+pre-existing -> logged as carry-forward (mode-guard, own PDR), NOT papered over in the PEER_STATUS hot path the PDR excluded. EMPIRICAL: tsc, 988 unit (+3), full Playwright 24/1, build 509.31KB. checkpoint d6b00ae.

P2 #join-attempt-mode-guard #defense-in-depth-reducer+shell #close-p1-check-residual: closed the P1 CHECK residual (invalid JOIN_ATTEMPT while mode!=select painting a wrong-mode red status) with a mode-guard. A.0 confirmed reachability: the dimmed joinButton stays eventMode=static (clickable) while hosting/joining though the HTML input is display:none outside select, so a button-click was the only non-select JOIN vector. FIX: reducer guard (mode!==select -> return state, same-ref no-op) + shell attemptJoin early-return (skips the wasted dispatch+applyView). KEY: behaviour-exact for ALL reachable flows since the UI only dispatches JOIN from select - the guard only changes the degenerate cross-pane path, a pure safety addition (47 ins/0 del). Grok break-it found CLEAN (3 attacks held); noted the dual-guard is redundant but harmless defense-in-depth (the reducer guard self-protects against any FUTURE direct dispatch, e.g. a network/auto-rejoin path). LESSON: when a UI affordance is visually disabled (dimmed) but still event-live, guard at the STATE layer (reducer), not just the visual layer - the reducer guard is the durable invariant. EMPIRICAL: tsc, 992 unit (+4), Playwright 24/1, build 509.37KB. checkpoint 3f5c33b.

P3 #ci-flake-harden #non-gating-quarantine-lane #grok-caught-report-collision+fail-unsafe-grep: quarantined the recurring 4-peer real-WebRTC nplayer CI-timeout flake to a non-gating lane so gating-CI-red == real regression. A.0 grounded the approach: retries=2 already existed (insufficient), deterministic-transport rejected by prior Council (config C9 - real P2P IS the surface), e2e.yml already deploy-independent -> the lever is a non-gating split, not a timeout bump or mock. FIX: e2e.yml Run-E2E split into gating (--grep-invert @quarantine-flaky) + non-gating (continue-on-error+if:always --grep @quarantine-flaky). KEY (Grok earned its keep a 3rd time): break-it r1 found TWO real issues my YAML-only first cut missed - (1) both lanes wrote the same playwright-report/+test-results/, so the non-gating lane (runs 2nd via if:always) OVERWROTE the gating artifact -> on a REAL regression the uploaded report would be the flaky test's (job status correct but evidence wrong); fixed via --output=test-results-quarantine + --reporter=list. (3) grepping the SEMANTIC title 4-player FFA was fail-UNSAFE - a future test sharing that phrase would silently non-gate; fixed via an explicit @quarantine-flaky tag (fail-SAFE: new tests gate by default, quarantine is opt-in). r2 verified fixes + 5 attacks held. ACCEPTED+logged (finding-2): sequential webServer :5173 boot race, non-gating-lane-only. LESSONS: (a) for run-but-non-gating you MUST split CI invocations - Playwright fails the whole run on any failed test regardless of tags. (b) BOOT-THEN-SMOKE a CI change: ran the EXACT npm-passthrough commands (--list + real gating + real quarantine) not just a YAML parse - static parse != runtime behaviour. (c) prefer fail-SAFE defaults (opt-in tag) over fail-unsafe (semantic match). (d) isolate per-lane output/reporter so a non-gating lane cannot corrupt the gating artifact. EMPIRICAL: --list 1/24, gating 23/1, quarantine 1 passed (34.8s). checkpoint cb0393c.

REAL-CI (gh run list/watch): P1 E2E success (6m45s) + Deploy success; P2 E2E success (5m53s) + Deploy success; P3 E2E SUCCESS (job green, 11m9s) - and the P3 run PROVED the quarantine mechanism in real CI: the gating lane passed 23/1 while the 4-peer quarantine step FAILED (exit 1, the known flake under CI network load) but continue-on-error kept the JOB GREEN. Exact designed behavior (gating-CI-red == real regression; a 4-peer flake no longer reds the gate), validated on the first real-CI run. NEW (logged, pre-existing): GitHub Actions Node20 deprecation warning (actions/checkout@v4 + setup-node@v4 + upload-artifact@v4) forced to Node24 ~2026-06-16; bump action versions in a future maintenance pass (out of S65 scope). No hallucinated flags, no non-existent refs, no unrendered vars. AUDIT PASS.
